states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Initial state after connection establishment, before user identification.

states_list.name: AUTH_PENDING
states_list.description: User identity provided, awaiting password verification.

states_list.name: AUTHENTICATED
states_list.description: Session successfully logged in and ready for operations.

states_list.name: DATA_CHANNEL_READY
states_list.description: Data connection parameters negotiated (via PASV/EPSV/PORT/EPRT), ready for transfer command.

states_list.name: DATA_TRANSFER
states_list.description: Active transfer of file data or directory listings.

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: A file rename source has been accepted, waiting for the destination name.

states_list.name: RESETTING
states_list.description: Session is being reinitialized, clearing user and state information.

invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
LIST, RETR, and STOR commands require the user to be in an authenticated state (following a successful PASS).
A PORT, PASV, EPRT, or EPSV command should be executed before a data transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE).
The QUIT command can be sent at any time to terminate the control connection.
If a PASS command fails (e.g., 530), the client may issue another USER command to retry authentication.
The RNTO command must be preceded immediately by an RNFR command.
FEAT can be called before navigation or data commands to determine server capabilities.
USER.username: Must be a valid username string
PASS.password: Must be a valid password string
TYPE.type_code: Commonly 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image (Binary)
PORT.address_port: Must be in format h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (comma-separated integers)
CWD.path: Path or directory name string
XCWD.path: Path or directory name string
RNFR.old_name: Name of file to be renamed
RNTO.new_name: New name for the file specified in preceding RNFR

ordering_rules: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
LIST, RETR, and STOR commands require the user to be in an authenticated state (following a successful PASS).
A PORT, PASV, EPRT, or EPSV command should be executed before a data transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE).
The QUIT command can be sent at any time to terminate the control connection.
If a PASS command fails (e.g., 530), the client may issue another USER command to retry authentication.
The RNTO command must be preceded immediately by an RNFR command.
FEAT can be called before navigation or data commands to determine server capabilities.
field_constraints: USER.username: Must be a valid username string
PASS.password: Must be a valid password string
TYPE.type_code: Commonly 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image (Binary)
PORT.address_port: Must be in format h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (comma-separated integers)
CWD.path: Path or directory name string
XCWD.path: Path or directory name string
RETR.filename: Filename string to retrieve
STOR.filename: Filename string to store
RNFR.old_name: Name of file to be renamed
RNTO.new_name: New name for the file specified in preceding RNFR

recommended_actions: Next iteration should emphasize broader data-channel and post-auth file-operation seeds to improve message-to-transition coverage.

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

fuzzing_observation.explanation: Next iteration should emphasize broader data-channel and post-auth file-operation seeds to improve message-to-transition coverage.

status_changes.rationale: USER command prompts for a password (331 response).
Correct password leads to successful login (230 response).
While usually following PASV/PORT, LIST can trigger a transfer using default settings, indicated by a 150 response.
Starting a directory listing transfer on a prepared channel (150 response).
Standard termination with 221 response.
State-preserving account information command.
State-preserving working directory query.
State-preserving directory change.
State-preserving directory change (parent).
State-preserving directory change.
State-preserving working directory query.
State-preserving directory change.
State-preserving directory creation.
State-preserving directory creation.
State-preserving directory removal.
State-preserving directory removal.
State-preserving file deletion.
State-preserving transfer type setting.
State-preserving transfer mode setting.
State-preserving file structure setting.
State-preserving structure mount.
State-preserving system type query.
State-preserving feature list query.
State-preserving help query.
State-preserving no-op.
State-preserving file information query (control channel).
State-preserving file size query.
State-preserving status query.
MLSD initiates a data transfer of directory information (150 response).
RETR initiates a data transfer of a file (150 response).
STOR initiates a data transfer of a file (150 response).
APPE initiates a data transfer (150 response).
MLSD initiates a directory list transfer (150 response).
APPE initiates a file append transfer (150 response).
Represents the return to the idle authenticated state upon completion of the LIST data transfer.
Represents the return to the idle authenticated state upon completion of the MLSD data transfer.
Represents the return to the idle authenticated state upon completion of the RETR data transfer.
Represents the return to the idle authenticated state upon completion of the STOR data transfer.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Per RFC 959, USER must be sent before PASS to identify the user for whom the password is provided.

status_changes.description: A PORT, PASV, EPRT, or EPSV command should be executed before a data transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE).
status_changes.rationale: A data connection must be established (active or passive) before data can be transferred.

status_changes.description: The RNTO command must be preceded immediately by an RNFR command.
status_changes.rationale: FTP rename sequence is strictly RNFR followed by RNTO.

status_changes.description: FEAT can be called before navigation or data commands to determine server capabilities.
status_changes.rationale: Modern FTP extensions (RFC 2389) allow FEAT to be called early to negotiate features.

status_changes.description: PORT.address_port: Must be in format h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (comma-separated integers)
status_changes.rationale: Defined format for the PORT command argument in RFC 959.

status_changes.description: CWD.path: Path or directory name string
status_changes.rationale: Standard parameter for directory change command.

status_changes.description: XCWD.path: Path or directory name string
status_changes.rationale: XCWD is the older/alternative form of CWD, taking the same argument type.
Required parameter for the RETR command.
Required parameter for the STOR command.

status_changes.description: RNFR.old_name: Name of file to be renamed
status_changes.rationale: Required parameter for the start of the rename sequence.

status_changes.description: RNTO.new_name: New name for the file specified in preceding RNFR
status_changes.rationale: Required parameter for the completion of the rename sequence.
